claim_text: Transaction Import & Approval System
source_quote: Transaction Import & Approval System

claim_text: Importers upload and review transaction files
source_quote: Importers to upload and review transaction files

claim_text: Enterprise financial transaction data management
source_quote: personal financial data

claim_text: Files are ingested via File Logs
source_quote: File-driven ingestion (via File Logs)

claim_text: Approvers review, approve/reject, and export transactions
source_quote: Approvers to review, approve/reject, and export transactions

claim_text: User and role administration is out of frontend MVP scope
source_quote: User and role administration — creating, updating, or deleting users and roles — is out of frontend scope for the MVP.

claim_text: Review of file-validation errors is out of frontend MVP scope
source_quote: Review of file-validation errors is out of frontend scope for the MVP.

claim_text: Represents an uploaded file and its processing state
source_quote: Represents an uploaded file and its processing state

claim_text: Represents individual records extracted from a file
source_quote: Represents individual records extracted from a file

claim_text: A per-file summary derived from a File Log and its Transactions
source_quote: Derived from FileLog + Transactions

claim_text: The two application roles are Importer and Approver
source_quote: The application's two roles are Importer and Approver, as defined in the brief; these are the canonical role vocabulary for role-gated screens.

claim_text: One File Log to many Transactions
source_quote: One **File Log → many Transactions**

claim_text: Actions on transactions affect status only, not structure
source_quote: Actions on transactions affect **status only**, not structure

claim_text: Imported, Approved, Rejected
source_quote: Imported / Approved / Rejected

claim_text: Terminal statuses Approved and Rejected are immutable
source_quote: Terminal transaction statuses (Approved and Rejected) are immutable: once a transaction is approved or rejected it cannot be re-actioned or reversed in the UI.

claim_text: Approve/reject only available while Imported
source_quote: Disable approve/reject if not

claim_text: Uploaded, Processing, Completed, Failed (inferred, provisional)
source_quote: The file-state set (Uploaded, Processing, Completed, Failed) is an inferred, provisional model rather than an authoritative state set

claim_text: Approve sets status to Approved
source_quote: Sets the Status of the transaction to 'Approved'.

claim_text: Reject sets status to Rejected and records the user note
source_quote: Sets the Status of the transaction to 'Rejected' and records the supplied user note.

claim_text: Importer uploads files
source_quote: Upload files

claim_text: Importer cannot approve/reject
source_quote: Cannot approve/reject

claim_text: Approver approves/rejects transactions
source_quote: Approve/reject

claim_text: Approver cannot upload
source_quote: Cannot upload

claim_text: User enters email and password
source_quote: User enters email + password

claim_text: Authentication endpoint is POST /v1/auth/login
source_quote: The authentication endpoint is POST /v1/auth/login, as defined in auth-api.yaml.

claim_text: Importers land on File Upload/File Log; Approvers land on Transaction Table
source_quote: Importers land on the File Upload / File Log area, and Approvers land on the Transaction Table.

claim_text: Backend creates a File Log on upload
source_quote: System creates FileLog

claim_text: Upload provides FileSettingId, FileSettingName, FileName
source_quote: FileSettingId

claim_text: File log overview uses GET /v1/file-logs
source_quote: GET /v1/file-logs

claim_text: File log columns: File Name, Process Date, Record Count, Status
source_quote: Record Count

claim_text: Row click drills into transactions
source_quote: Row click → drill into transactions

claim_text: Transaction table uses GET /v1/transactions
source_quote: GET /v1/transactions

claim_text: Reject requires a mandatory note
source_quote: Enter **mandatory note**

claim_text: Export applies current filters
source_quote: Applies current filters

claim_text: Default export format is CSV
source_quote: CSV (default)

claim_text: Client-side filtering over the full list; CSV export from the filtered grid
source_quote: Transaction-table filtering (by Status, File, Date, Amount, and free text) is performed client-side over the full list returned by the transactions endpoint, and CSV export is generated from the filtered grid.

claim_text: Empty filtered set shows a no-op state
source_quote: shows a no-op state when the filtered set is empty

claim_text: Client-side pagination over the full transaction list
source_quote: the transaction list applies a sensible default of client-side pagination (or virtualisation) over the full list

claim_text: An already-actioned transaction cannot be actioned again; the second user sees the changed status
source_quote: a transaction that has already been actioned cannot be actioned again, and the second user is shown that its status has changed

claim_text: New status appears within about one second
source_quote: the transaction's new status appears in the UI within about one second

claim_text: Transaction status is shown as a status chip
source_quote: Transaction status is shown in the UI as a status chip

claim_text: Search by Reference and Account, plus status/file/date/amount filters
source_quote: Text search (Reference, Account)

claim_text: File summary counts by status
source_quote: Count by status:

claim_text: AccountNumber masked on screen showing only the last group of digits
source_quote: AccountNumber is masked on screen by default (showing only its last group of digits), reflecting POPIA-aligned handling of personal financial data.

claim_text: Desktop browsers first; baseline accessibility standard
source_quote: The application targets desktop browsers first and meets a baseline accessibility standard

claim_text: The brief's seven named key screens are the in-scope MVP set
source_quote: The brief's seven named key screens are the explicit in-scope MVP set

claim_text: Transaction model includes Description and TransactionType
source_quote: The Transaction model the UI displays includes Description and TransactionType in addition to the brief's listed attributes

claim_text: TransactionType is the full words Credit and Debit
source_quote: TransactionType is represented by the full words 'Credit' and 'Debit'

claim_text: TransactionDate canonical format YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm
source_quote: TransactionDate is displayed and parsed in a single canonical format — YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm

claim_text: AccountNumber canonical representation is plain digits
source_quote: AccountNumber has one canonical representation in the UI — plain digits, matching the API example

claim_text: RecordCount canonical type is integer
source_quote: FileLog.RecordCount represents a count and its canonical type is integer

claim_text: Transaction fields: Reference, TransactionDate, AccountNumber, Description, Amount, TransactionType, Currency
source_quote: Reference,TransactionDate,AccountNumber,Description,Amount,TransactionType,Currency

claim_text: Transaction carries a UserNote
source_quote: UserNote

claim_text: File Log carries a FileName
source_quote: FileName

claim_text: Currency e.g. ZAR
source_quote: ZAR

claim_text: The frontend displays and surfaces; it does not enforce
source_quote: the frontend displays and surfaces them (it is read-oriented); it does not mean the frontend enforces them.

claim_text: Upload a file operation
source_quote: Upload a file

claim_text: Approve a transaction by Id operation
source_quote: Approve a transaction by Id

claim_text: Reject a transaction by Id operation
source_quote: Reject a transaction by Id

claim_text: Session state via an HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=Strict cookie
source_quote: Session state is conveyed exclusively via an HttpOnly, Secure,

claim_text: On failure, a generic 401 message that does not reveal which field was incorrect
source_quote: On failure, returns 401 with a deliberately generic message that

claim_text: Process-definition management is out of frontend MVP scope
source_quote: Process-definition management is out of frontend scope for the MVP

claim_text: NFRs, KPIs, deadlines, compliance signals are stamped from domain defaults
source_quote: non-functional requirements, KPIs, deadlines, and compliance signals are not supplied by the corpus and are stamped from domain defaults

claim_text: File Status derived from LastExecutedActivityName via an activity-name-to-status mapping
source_quote: The file Status is derived from the LastExecutedActivityName field via an explicit mapping from activity names to the file-status set.

claim_text: User-facing recovery flow for a Failed file is unspecified
source_quote: The user-facing recovery flow for a Failed file is unspecified and is to be treated as not-yet-defined

claim_text: Both roles view transactions
source_quote: View transactions

claim_text: Both roles view file summaries
source_quote: View file summaries
